ProductionLoss
==================================================

.. math::

   \text{}

.. raw:: html

      <a href="/edit?kpi=ProductionLoss" target="_blank" class="btn btn-edit">Edit</a>
   </div>

   <table class="kpi-table">
      <tbody>
         <tr>
            <td>Description</td>
            <td><ul><li>Production Loss measures the difference between the planned output and the actual output in a production process</li><li>It highlights inefficiencies and areas where production targets are not met</li></ul></td>
         </tr>
         <tr>
            <td>Objective</td>
            <td><ul><li>The objective of tracking Production Loss is to identify gaps between expected and actual production levels</li><li>enabling organizations to implement corrective actions and improve overall operational efficiency</li></ul></td>
         </tr>
         <tr>
            <td>Formula</td>
            <td>
               <p>Production Loss is calculated by subtracting the total actual output from the total planned output over a specified period.</p>
               <div class="math-equation">$$ \mathit{Production\,Loss} = \mathit{Sum(Production[Planned\,Output])} - \mathit{Sum(Production[Actual\,Output])} $$</div>
            </td>
         </tr>

         <tr>
            <td>Generation time</td>
            <td>
               0.0s (AI 0.0s + Render 0.0s)
               <div style="font-size: 12px; color: #6b7280; margin-top: 4px;">
                  Total: 0.0s
                  • AI: 0.0s
                  • Render: 0.0s
               </div>
            </td>
         </tr>

         <tr>
            <td>Extraction rate %</td>
            <td>100.0%</td>
         </tr>
         <tr>
            <td>Error rate %</td>
            <td>0.0%</td>
         </tr>
         <tr>
            <td>Used in KPI(s)</td>
            <td><ul><li>Production Loss is utilized in various KPIs related to operational efficiency</li><li>production performance</li><li>and resource management</li></ul></td>
         </tr>
         <tr>
            <td>Input Measure</td>
            <td><ul><li>The input measures for this KPI include both planned output and actual output from production activities</li></ul></td>
         </tr>
         <tr>
            <td>Unit of Measure</td>
            <td>The unit of measure for Production Loss is typically expressed in units of output, such as barrels for oil and gas production or tons for petrochemical production.</td>
         </tr>
         <tr>
            <td>Reporting Source</td>
            <td>The reporting source for this KPI is derived from production data collected during operational reporting cycles.</td>
         </tr>
         <tr>
            <td>Comments</td>
            <td>Monitoring Production Loss is crucial for continuous improvement initiatives and can significantly impact profitability and resource allocation.</td>
         </tr>
      </tbody>
   </table>

.. admonition:: Code Context
   :class: dropdown

   
   
   

   .. code-block:: text
      :linenos:
   

      -- KPI074: # Production Loss
   ProductionLoss = SUM(Production[PlannedOutput]) - SUM(Production[ActualOutput])
